_comment: Game content for the 4 rounds. Round 2 photos load live from Wikipedia (freely-licensed lead images) using the 'wiki' title. Round 3 'audioUrl' is null by default because famous broadcast calls are copyrighted — plug in your own licensed clips, or leave null to play from the transcript. Regenerate/extend with tools/fetch_content.py.

questions.q: How many World Series championships have the Yankees won?
questions.fact: 27 titles — the most of any North American pro sports franchise.

questions.q: What number did Derek Jeter wear?
questions.fact: No. 2 — the last of the Yankees' single-digit numbers to be retired.

questions.q: Which Yankee is nicknamed 'The Sultan of Swat'?
questions.fact: Babe Ruth also went by the Bambino and the Great Bambino.

questions.q: What is the Yankees' home ballpark called?
questions.fact: The current Yankee Stadium opened in 2009, across the street from the original 'House That Ruth Built.'

questions.q: Mariano Rivera is famous for playing which position?
questions.fact: Rivera's 652 saves are the most in MLB history — all with one pitch, the cutter.

questions.q: How long was Joe DiMaggio's record hitting streak?
questions.fact: 56 straight games in 1941 — a record many consider unbreakable.
Gehrig's 'luckiest man' speech in 1939 is one of sport's most famous moments.
Berra won 10 World Series rings as a player — an MLB record.
Maris hit 61 homers in 1961 to break Babe Ruth's single-season record.
Bernie is also an accomplished jazz guitarist with a Latin Grammy nomination.
'Louisiana Lightning' went 25-3 with a 1.74 ERA in his 1978 Cy Young season.
Munson was the first Yankees captain since Lou Gehrig.

questions.prompt: Identify the moment from this famous call
questions.transcript: "There it goes... and it is... HIGH! It is FAR! It is... GONE! Number 61!"
questions.fact: Maris broke a 34-year-old record held by his own teammate's predecessor, Babe Ruth.

questions.transcript: "An out away... Rivera coming set... and the Yankees are World Champions again!"
choices: Any of the above — Rivera closed them all
questions.fact: Rivera was on the mound for the final out of the 1998, 1999, 2000 and 2009 clinchers.

questions.transcript: "He hits it deep to left... it is high enough... GONE! The Yankees win it in extra innings — Mr. November!"
questions.fact: Jeter's walk-off in Game 4 came just after midnight on Nov 1, 2001 — earning the 'Mr. November' nickname.

questions.transcript: "Chambliss swings... deep to right-center... it's GONE! The Yankees are going to the World Series!"
questions.fact: Chambliss's walk-off homer sent the Yankees to their first World Series in 12 years.

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Red Sox never sold Babe Ruth to the Yankees in 1919?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: how many of the Yankees' 27 titles still happen?
options: About the same — the dynasty finds another way
Very few — Ruth was the spark
Zero — no Curse of the Bambino, no dynasty

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Yankees had NOT signed CC Sabathia and Mark Teixeira before 2009?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: do they still win the 2009 World Series?
options: No, those signings were the difference

whatIfs.scenario: What if Mickey Mantle had stayed fully healthy his whole career?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: where does he rank all-time?
options: Elite but roughly the same legacy

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Yankees traded for a young Shohei Ohtani in his prime?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: how many titles in the next 5 years?
